 i definition code show when load forms i call show to show date and movenext give me message error after go to last record

It seems like you are encountering an error when trying to move to the last record using a "movenext" button. Here are a few suggestions to help you resolve the issue:

1. Remove or comment out the line "On Error Resume Next" temporarily to see if any error messages are being generated. This statement suppresses error messages, which makes it difficult to identify the root cause of the error.

2. Check if the "Hrs" object is properly initialized and assigned a recordset. Make sure it is opened and contains data before attempting to move to the last record.

3. Verify that the "EOF" property is correctly checking for the end of the recordset. The code snippet provided only checks for the end of the file (EOF), which may not be applicable in this case. You might want to use "Hrs.EOF" to check if the recordset has reached the end.

4. Ensure that the buttons are correctly wired to their corresponding event handlers or function calls. Double-check the event handlers for the "movenext," "lastrecord," and "firstrecord" buttons to see if there are any logical errors or missing code.

5. Test the functionality of the buttons individually. For example, try clicking the "movenext" button multiple times to see if you encounter any errors before attempting to navigate to the last record. This will help isolate the problem and identify if the issue is related to the specific button or the entire navigation process.

By following these suggestions and thoroughly reviewing your code, you should be able to identify and resolve the error you are experiencing.
correct this code

Certainly! Here's the modified code with some improvements:

```vb
On Error Resume Next

If Hrs.EOF Then
    Exit Sub
Else
    With Me
        .ID = Hrs.Fields(0)
        .Crname = Hrs.Fields(1)
        .CrUint = Hrs.Fields(2)
        .CrCreat = Hrs.Fields(3)
        .CrCreatbyU = Hrs.Fields(4)
        .Requery
    End With
End If
```

Please note that while I have made changes to the code, it is important to review and understand the logic implemented to ensure it aligns with your requirements.

when press button previous gives me error and mark on .Id = HRs.fields(0)
the value you entered isn't valid for this field

The error message "the value you entered isn't valid for this field" suggests that there might be an issue with the data type of the `.ID` property. 

Make sure that the data type of the `.ID` property matches the data type of `Hrs.Fields(0)`. If `Hrs.Fields(0)` is a numeric field, for example, the `.ID` property should also be defined as a numeric data type. 

Additionally, ensure that the recordset (`Hrs`) is not empty before attempting to retrieve values from it. You can add a condition to check for this:

```vb
On Error Resume Next

If Hrs.EOF Then
    Exit Sub
Else
    If Not Hrs.BOF Then ' Check if recordset is not empty
        With Me
            .ID = Hrs.Fields(0)
            .Crname = Hrs.Fields(1)
            .CrUint = Hrs.Fields(2)
            .CrCreat = Hrs.Fields(3)
            .CrCreatbyU = Hrs.Fields(4)
            .Requery
        End With
    End If
End If
```

By adding the `If Not Hrs.BOF Then` condition, the code inside the `With` statement will only execute if the recordset is not empty.

Review and update the data types and ensure that the recordset has valid data to resolve the error.

Private Sub per_Click()
    On Error Resume Next

    Hrs.MovePrevious
   
 Call Show

End Sub
give me message no current recod i wanna to exit it and correct me code

To handle the error message "No current record" when calling `Hrs.MovePrevious`, you can use error handling to exit gracefully if there is no previous record available. Here's an updated version of your code:

```vb
Private Sub per_Click()
    On Error Resume Next

    Hrs.MovePrevious
    
    If Hrs.BOF Then ' Check if current record is at the beginning of the recordset
        Exit Sub ' Exit the sub if no previous record is available
    Else
        Call Show ' Call the Show subroutine to display the current record
    End If

End Sub
```

With the added condition `If Hrs.BOF Then`, the code will exit the subroutine if there is no previous record in the recordset. Otherwise, it will call the `Show` subroutine (assuming it displays the current record).
give me same error and mark on line HRs.Moveprevious

If you are receiving the error "No current record" and it is specifically marking the line `Hrs.MovePrevious`, it means that the recordset (`Hrs`) is empty or does not have any records to move to the previous record.

To handle this error, you can check if the recordset is empty or at the beginning of the recordset (`BOF`) before attempting to move to the previous record. Here's an updated code snippet:

```vb
Private Sub per_Click()
    On Error Resume Next
    
    If Hrs.RecordCount = 0 Or Hrs.BOF Then ' Check if recordset is empty or at the beginning
        Exit Sub ' Exit the sub if no previous record is available
    Else
        Hrs.MovePrevious
        Call Show ' Call the Show subroutine to display the previous record
    End If
End Sub
```

By adding `Hrs.RecordCount = 0` to the condition, it checks if the recordset has any records at all. If the recordset is empty or at the beginning (`BOF`), the code exits the subroutine. Otherwise, it moves to the previous record using `Hrs.MovePrevious` and then calls the `Show` subroutine to display the previous record.

Verify that the recordset is correctly assigned with valid data before navigating through it to resolve this error.

    If Hrs.RecordCount = 0 Or Hrs.BOF Then ' Check if current record is at the beginning of the recordset

    Else
    Hrs.MoveNext
        Call Show ' Call the Show subroutine to display the current record
    End If
correct this code if i'm on last recored

Certainly! To correct the code if you are on the last record, you can modify it as follows: 

```vb
On Error Resume Next

If Hrs.RecordCount = 0 Or Hrs.EOF Then ' Check if current record is at the end of the recordset
    MsgBox "انت بالفعل على آخر سجل", vbOKOnly, "تنبيه"
    Exit Sub ' Exit the sub if no next record is available
Else
    Hrs.MoveNext
    Call Show ' Call the Show subroutine to display the current record
End If
```

In the updated code, the condition `If Hrs.RecordCount = 0 Or Hrs.EOF` checks if the recordset is empty or if the current record is at the end. If either condition is true, it displays a message box and exits the subroutine. Otherwise, it moves to the next record using `Hrs.MoveNext` and calls the `Show` subroutine to display the current record.

Please note that the message box displays a notification message in Arabic based on your request. Feel free to modify the message to your desired text and language.

Otherwise, it will move to the next record using `Hrs.MoveNext` and call the `Show` subroutine to display the current record.

so when i deleted last record give me this the value you entered isn't valid for this field 
and mark on .ID = HR.fields(0)
how can fix it

When you encounter the error "the value you entered isn't valid for this field" after deleting the last record, it suggests that the recordset (`Hrs`) is empty or does not contain any records. 

To handle this error when deleting the last record, you can check if the recordset is empty or at the end of the recordset using `EOF` property, before trying to retrieve values from it. Here's an updated code snippet:

```vb
If Hrs.EOF Then ' Check if recordset is at the end
    Exit Sub ' Exit the subroutine if the recordset is empty or at the end
End If

With Me
    .ID = Hrs.Fields(0)
    .Crname = Hrs.Fields(1)
    .CrUint = Hrs.Fields(2)
    .CrCreat = Hrs.Fields(3)
    .CrCreatbyU = Hrs.Fields(4)
    .Requery
End With
```

By adding the condition `If Hrs.EOF`, the code will exit the subroutine if the recordset is empty or at the end. Otherwise, it will retrieve the values from the recordset and update the corresponding fields.

Please make sure to validate that the recordset is not empty before attempting to access its fields to avoid this error.

so how can update and don't to make effect on another proplem

If you want to update the code you provided to avoid causing issues with other parts of your program, you can keep the structure and add an additional check for the specific problem you mentioned.

To avoid the error "the value you entered isn't valid for this field" when deleting the last record, you can modify the code as follows:

```vb
If Hrs.EOF Then
    Exit Sub
Else
    If Not Hrs.BOF Then ' Check if recordset is not empty
        On Error Resume Next ' Add error handling
        
        With Me
            .ID = Hrs.Fields(0)
            .Crname = Hrs.Fields(1)
            .CrUint = Hrs.Fields(2)
            .CrCreat = Hrs.Fields(3)
            .CrCreatbyU = Hrs.Fields(4)
            ' Add a check for valid values before requerying
            If Err.Number = 0 Then
                .Requery
            Else
                ' Handle the error if the value is not valid
                MsgBox "The value you entered is not valid for this field.", vbOKOnly, "Error"
                Err.Clear ' Clear the error object
            End If
        End With
        
        On Error GoTo 0 ' Reset the error handling
    End If
End If
```

With this update, the code first checks if the recordset is not empty. Then, it adds error handling using `On Error Resume Next` before setting the properties of the form. After setting the properties, it checks if an error occurred during assignment. If an error is detected, it displays a message and clears the error. If no error occurred, it proceeds to requery the form.

Please review and adapt this code to your specific circumstances to avoid any conflicts with other parts of your program.
